title: microsorum congregatifolium
intro: Microsorum congregatifolium, also known as Congregate Fern or Mua Leong, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Polypodiaceae. This plant is traditionally used in Southeast Asian medicine, particularly in Malaysia and Thailand, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Microsorum congregatifolium include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and cholesterol levels. Microsorum congregatifolium is often prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, and in some cases, it is also used as a poultice or applied topically to affected areas.
